application_credentials.description:
Open your Tesla Developer App in the [Tesla Developer Portal]({dashboard}). Store the client ID and client secret in Home Assistant and also register exactly this OAuth redirect URI in the Tesla app:

`{redirect_uri}`

Allowed origin/public-key domain is a separate Tesla value and does not replace the redirect URI.

oauth_redirect_info.title: Check Tesla OAuth redirect URI
oauth_redirect_info.description:
Tesla accepts login only when the redirect URI is registered exactly for the client ID being used.

Add this redirect URI to your app in the [Tesla Developer Portal]({dashboard}):

`{redirect_uri}`

Save the Tesla app and wait briefly before continuing. Allowed origin/public-key domain is also required, but it does not replace this redirect URI.

oauth_preflight_failed.title: Check Tesla OAuth settings
oauth_preflight_failed.description:
The Tesla OAuth preflight check failed.

Client ID from Home Assistant:

`{client_id}`

Redirect URI used by Home Assistant:

`{redirect_uri}`

Correct these values in the [Tesla Developer Portal]({dashboard}), save the app, and wait a few minutes. Then continue again.

Tesla hint: `{detail}`

oauth_preflight_warning.title: Tesla OAuth preflight unavailable
oauth_preflight_warning.description:
Home Assistant could not complete the Tesla OAuth preflight check right now.

Client ID from Home Assistant:

`{client_id}`

Redirect URI used by Home Assistant:

`{redirect_uri}`

Check these values in the [Tesla Developer Portal]({dashboard}). If they are correct, you can continue; otherwise correct them first.

Technical detail: `{detail}`

domain_input.title: Register Tesla Fleet domain
domain_input.description: Enter the public domain that hosts the Tesla public key. This domain must be configured as an allowed origin in the Tesla Developer Portal: {dashboard}

domain_registration.title: Host Tesla public key
domain_registration.description:
The integration serves the public key automatically at `{public_key_url}` when this domain points to Home Assistant. Check the URL from outside your network; it must return HTTP 200 and start with `-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----`.

If the domain does not point directly to Home Assistant, place this public key there exactly:

```text
{pem}
```

registration_complete.title: Pair Virtual Key
registration_complete.description:
Open this link and add the Virtual Key in the Tesla app so signed vehicle commands can work:

{virtual_key_url}

reauth_confirm.title: Reconnect Tesla Fleet
reauth_confirm.description: Tesla requires a new login for {name}.

error.invalid_domain: Invalid domain. Enter only the host name without https:// and without a path.
error.invalid_response: Tesla did not return a valid partner registration response.
error.oauth_error: Tesla Fleet OAuth failed. Check client ID, client secret and allowed origin.
error.oauth_preflight_client_id: Tesla does not recognize this client ID. Check that the client ID and client secret come from the same Tesla Developer App.
error.oauth_preflight_failed: Tesla rejected the OAuth request. Check client ID, redirect URI, grant types and scopes.
error.oauth_preflight_redirect_uri: Tesla rejected the redirect URI for this client ID. Add the displayed redirect URI exactly as an allowed redirect URI in the Tesla Developer App.
error.oauth_preflight_unreachable: Tesla OAuth could not be reached for the preflight check.
error.precondition_failed: Tesla rejected the domain. Check the allowed origin and public-key URL in the Tesla Developer Portal.
error.public_key_mismatch: The public key read by Tesla does not match the local key.
error.public_key_not_found: Tesla could not fetch a public key for this domain.
